classifications.phrase: inflation continues to moderate, in line with the RBAs latest forecasts
continuing excess demand in the economy and strong domestic cost pressures
Higher interest rates are working to establish a more sustainable balance
conditions in the labour market continue to ease gradually
remain tighter than is consistent with sustained full employment and inflation at target
Wages growth picked up a little further in the December quarter
appears to have peaked with indications it will moderate over the year ahead
encouraging signs that inflation is moderating
Household consumption growth remains particularly weak
growth in unit labour costs remains very high
inflation to return to the target range of 2–3 per cent in 2025
unemployment rate and the broader underutilisation rate are expected to increase a bit further
services price inflation has remained persistent
Returning inflation to target within a reasonable timeframe remains the Boards highest priority
inflation is easing, it remains high
it will be some time yet before inflation is sustainably in the target range
The Board is not ruling anything in or out
Board remains resolute in its determination to return inflation to target

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance by emphasizing persistent inflation concerns, excess demand, and strong cost pressures, while maintaining the cash rate unchanged and reserving the option for future tightening.
